summary: credits reports the real plan name (plan.name / plan.plan_key) and surfaces the billing period separately, instead of mislabeling the billing interval as the plan.
reason: Suno's live /api/billing/info/ response nests the plan under a 'plan' object (plan.name = 'Premier Plan', plan.plan_key = 'premier'), sets subscription_type to a bool, and carries the billing interval in 'period' (e.g. 'year'). parseCredits only matched string-valued keys in the order {plan, subscription_type, subscription, tier, period}: the plan object and bool subscription_type were skipped, so 'period' won and the command reported plan='year'. planName now reads the nested plan object first (name, then plan_key), falls back to legacy top-level string shapes, and excludes 'period'; period is reported in its own field. Verified live: {credits:8855, plan:'Premier Plan', period:'year'}.
